forecast-period.text: A high pressure system centred over the south of SA will move slowly eastwards this week and a cold front will move across the far southeast of SA on Thursday. A second high pressure system is expected to follow and will become established south of the Bight late Thursday then will move eastwards during Friday and Saturday. A trough will move over the far west on Saturday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning fog patches in the south. Showers about the Lower South East, and isolated showers possible about remaining southern coasts. Sunny in the north. Maximum temperatures slightly above average in the far southwest but near or slightly below average elsewhere. Mostly light northeast to northwesterly winds. Afternoon sea breezes near the coast.

Morning fog patches about Eyre Peninsula. Isolated showers about southern coastal districts, more frequent about the Lower South East district. Mostly sunny in the north. Maximum temperatures near or slightly above average. Northwesterly winds in the south, freshening near southern coasts, ahead of a southwesterly change in the afternoon. Generally light winds in the north.

Morning fog patches about Eyre Peninsula. Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, clearing during the evening. Isolated showers developing in the far west. Maximum temperatures below average about the agricultural area but near or slightly above average elsewhere. South to southeasterly winds, tending southeast to northeasterly in the west.

text.p: Isolated showers in the west. Maximum temperatures below average in the east but above average in the west. Northeast to northwesterly winds, shifting southwest to southeasterly in the far southwest later.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Saturday: less than 2 mm about the southern agricultural area and West Coast district, increasing to local falls of 2 to 5 mm about southern coasts and ranges and the Lower South East district. Isolated falls of 5 to 10 mm are possible about the Lower South East.
